name: INFANTILE FIBROMATOSIS OF ORBIT
definition: rare nonmetastasizing locally invasive fibrous proliferation with tendency to recur after

demographics.Location: head & neck > trunk > lower extremity > upper extremity

dominant_finding: ± orbital bone erosion ± small foci of calcifications
distribution: head & neck > trunk > lower extremity > upper extremity
discriminating_features: ± orbital bone erosion ± small foci of calcifications
low to intermediate SI relative to muscle on T1WI + (frequently) foci of hyperintensity

US: round well-circumscribed mass of heterogeneous texture
CT: ± orbital bone erosion ± small foci of calcifications
MR: low to intermediate SI relative to muscle on T1WI + (frequently) foci of hyperintensity
heterogeneous predominantly hyperintense SI on T2WI:
T2 hyperintensity ← high cellularity + myxoid change
target appearance ← central hemorrhage / necrosis / cystic / myxoid change

clinical.Prognosis: spontaneous resolution (common); 7% recurrence rate after surgical resection
clinical.Rx: conservative; surgical resection for local complications
